title: The census scope was one directory too narrow — widen it to every caller, and fix the fifth site it was hiding
action: jrt-1's census scans `.bee/bin/lib/**` and `templates/lib/**`. That scope was written by the orchestrator from assumption, not measurement, and it was too narrow: a repo-wide grep for `logDecision(` finds callers in `.bee/bin/bee.mjs` and its template twin `skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs` as well. MEASURED, both sites: `bee.mjs:1468` is the CLI's own `decisions log` handler and correctly FORWARDS user-supplied tags (`tags: flags.tags !== undefined ? splitList(flags.tags) : undefined`) — leave it alone. `bee.mjs:1880` is the scribing-debt WAIVER record written on feature close and carries NO tags, so it throws typed DECISIONS_UNTAGGED_REFUSED in any repo with a taxonomy. It is the worst-placed instance of the five: it is the audit record that is the stated PRICE of the sanctioned waiver door, and the comment directly above it says the decision is written after the state lock releases — so a throw there leaves the close half-done, state already mutated and the record that explains why absent. STEP 1 — WIDEN THE CENSUS to every non-test caller under `.bee/bin/**` and `skills/bee-hive/templates/**`, still DERIVED by scanning source, never a path list maintained by hand. Test fixtures under `templates/tests/` stay excluded (a fixture's untagged call is its subject matter) — exclude them structurally, and say why in the code. RED-FIRST: run the widened census BEFORE the fix and capture it naming bee.mjs:1880 in both trees. STEP 2 — fix that site with tags from the EXISTING taxonomy vocabulary (`scribing`, `cells`, `state`, `gates` are all present — pick what the event IS; do not invent). STEP 3 — the same line carries a SECOND, separate defect found while reading it: its decision text ends 'Their settled behavior is NOT in docs/specs/.' unconditionally, which is the retired state layer. In a repo with a bundle that sentence is simply false. Make it name the resolved target the way the session-close nudge now does (see hooks/bee-session-close.mjs for the accepted shape). NOTE: `.bee/bin/**` is outside the instruction fence's surfaces, which is why no gate caught this — say so in a comment beside the fix rather than silently repairing it. STEP 4 — mirror `.bee/bin/bee.mjs` and `templates/bee.mjs` byte-identical, then the four derived regens (mirror, plugin render, release manifest, self-onboard), then the full chain BOTH ways as the last action.

truths: red evidence: the WIDENED census, run before the fix, naming bee.mjs:1880 in both trees — quoted
green evidence: the waiver path succeeds in a taxonomy fixture and lands a tagged event; the census is green
the widened census still bites — inject a tagless call into bee.mjs and show it fires, then restore
bee.mjs:1468 is confirmed UNCHANGED — it forwards user tags and was never broken
the waiver's decision text names the resolved state layer, not docs/specs unconditionally
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
prohibitions: never weaken the untagged refusal, and never pass a catch-all tag to satisfy it
do not hand-maintain a path list — the census derives its scope by scanning
do not exclude bee.mjs from the census to make it pass
do not touch the CLI handler at bee.mjs:1468

trace.outcome: The census I wrote one cell ago was scoped from assumption instead of measurement, and it was one directory too narrow — green on a tree that still carried a live instance of the exact bug it exists to prevent. Widened to every non-test caller under .bee/bin/** and templates/**, still derived by scanning source, with test fixtures excluded structurally and the reason stated. Run red first it named the fifth site: the scribing-debt waiver's audit record, the worst-placed of the five, because it is written after the state lock releases — a throw there leaves a feature closed with its state mutated and the record explaining the waiver absent. Fixed with existing taxonomy tags. The CLI's own decisions-log handler is confirmed untouched and correctly unflagged, since forcing a tag there would fake a value that belongs to the user. Reading that line also turned up a second defect no gate could see: its permanent decision text asserted unconditionally that waived behavior is not in the retired doc tree, which is false in a bundle repo — now resolved through the predicate, with a comment recording that .bee/bin/** sits outside the instruction fence's surfaces, so the hole is documented rather than quietly patched.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "The WIDENED census, run before the fix, named the fifth site in both trees: 'every internal logDecision( call must carry a tags: array (docs/decisions/taxonomy.json makes an untagged event a hard refusal) — offenders: .bee/bin/bee.mjs:1879, skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs:1879'. jrt-1's census, scoped to lib/** only, was green on the very same tree — which is the finding: the scope was written from assumption and hid a live instance of the exact bug it was built to prevent.",
  "why_this_site_is_the_worst_of_the_five": "It is the audit record that is the stated PRICE of the sanctioned scribing-debt waiver, and the comment above it notes the decision is written AFTER the state lock releases. A throw there leaves the close half-done: state already mutated to compounding-complete, and the record explaining why the debt was waived absent. The waiver door would appear to have been used with no trace of its justification.",
  "green_evidence": "Census green across the widened scope. Two new proof tests in test_bee_cli.mjs: a taxonomy-enforced fixture showing the waiver close SUCCEEDS and lands tags ['scribing','state'], and a bundle fixture showing the decision text names the bundle instead of the unconditional retired path. Chain 81 suites, both ways, re-run independently by the orchestrator.",
  "census_still_bites": "Injection proof: a tagless logDecision call was added to .bee/bin/bee.mjs and the census failed immediately naming .bee/bin/bee.mjs:1498 — the injected line. The file was restored and confirmed byte-identical to its template twin afterwards.",
  "the_site_that_must_not_be_flagged": "bee.mjs's own `decisions log` CLI handler forwards user-supplied tags and was never broken. Confirmed byte-identical after all edits (git diff shows zero hunks touching it), and correctly NOT flagged by the census, because it always passes a tags key — even when undefined — which the scanner recognises as present. A census that flagged it would have pushed an author to fake a tag on a path whose tags are the user's to choose.",
  "second_defect_on_the_same_line": "Found by reading the line rather than by any gate: the waiver's decision text ended 'Their settled behavior is NOT in docs/specs/.' unconditionally — false in any repo with a bundle, and it would have been written into the permanent decision log as fact. Now resolved through the same predicate, mirroring the shape the session-close nudge already uses. A comment beside the fix records WHY no gate caught it: .bee/bin/** sits outside the instruction fence's covered surfaces. That is a known, now-documented hole rather than a silent repair.",
  "tags_not_invented": "['scribing','state'] — both already in docs/decisions/taxonomy.json (scribing: 'Spec sync and BA capture'; state: 'Runtime state and phases'). The event is a scribing-debt waiver recorded at a feature-close phase transition, so both describe what it IS.",
  "known_limitation_of_the_census": "The scanner matches the call token textually and has no comment-awareness beyond skipping the function declaration itself, so a COMMENT containing the call token reads as a call site. The worker hit this with its own explanatory comment and reworded the comment rather than loosening the scanner — the correct direction, and worth knowing before someone writes about this code and is puzzled by a red.",
  "chain": "Re-run INDEPENDENTLY by the orchestrator: BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 81 exit 0; node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 81 exit 0. Mirror byte-identity between .bee/bin/bee.mjs and templates/bee.mjs confirmed by direct diff.",
  "orchestrator_review": "One flagged diagnostic investigated and dismissed: an unused `root` parameter reported at bee.mjs:471 is PRE-EXISTING (same function, same signature at HEAD line 470) and merely shifted one line by the new import. Not introduced here."
}
